(born 20 November 1987) is a [Welsh](/wiki/Wales "Wales") [rugby union](/wiki/Rugby_union "Rugby union") player. Lewis primarily played for [Newport Gwent Dragons](/wiki/Newport_Gwent_Dragons "Newport Gwent Dragons"), [London Welsh RFC](/wiki/London_Welsh_RFC "London Welsh RFC"), and [Birmingham Moseley](/wiki/Birmingham_Moseley_Rugby "Birmingham Moseley Rugby"). Lewis represented Wales at grade age levels, as well as in rugby sevens.

["Rob Lewis is back with London Welsh on loan from Cardiff Blues"](https://www.skysports.com/rugby-union/news/15189/8345170/rob-lewis-is-back-with-london-welsh-on-loan-from-cardiff-blues). *Sky Sports*. 20 December 2012. Retrieved 9 May 2023.

["Travelling Lucs fans spur side to local derby victory"](https://www.herefordtimes.com/sport/18117999.travelling-luctonians-fans-spur-side-local-derby-victory/). *Hereford Times*. 22 December 2019. Retrieved 9 May 2023.

**Robert Lewis** (born 20 November 1987) is a [Welsh](/wiki/Wales "Wales") [rugby union](/wiki/Rugby_union "Rugby union") player. Lewis primarily played for [Newport Gwent Dragons](/wiki/Newport_Gwent_Dragons "Newport Gwent Dragons"), [London Welsh RFC](/wiki/London_Welsh_RFC "London Welsh RFC"), and [Birmingham Moseley](/wiki/Birmingham_Moseley_Rugby "Birmingham Moseley Rugby"). Lewis represented Wales at grade age levels, as well as in rugby sevens.

## Professional career

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Robert_Lewis_(rugby_union)&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: Professional career")]

A [scrum-half](/wiki/Scrum-half_(rugby_union) "Scrum-half (rugby union)"), he has represented Wales at U18, U19 and at U21 level, and was selected for the [Wales Sevens](/wiki/Wales_national_rugby_sevens_team "Wales national rugby sevens team") squad in 2007, in which he competed in four tournaments, – [Adelaide](/wiki/Adelaide "Adelaide"), [Wellington](/wiki/Wellington "Wellington"), [Hong Kong](/wiki/Hong_Kong "Hong Kong") and San Diego. He was a key figure for Wales U19s, helping them win the Six Nations Championship [Grand Slam](/wiki/Grand_Slam_(rugby_union) "Grand Slam (rugby union)") and was Wales's top [try](/wiki/Try_(rugby) "Try (rugby)") scorer in the tournament. The same year he represented Wales U19 at the Junior World Cup in [Dubai](/wiki/Dubai "Dubai").

Robert came through the Newport Gwent Dragons Academy learning his trade with [Ebbw Vale RFC](/wiki/Ebbw_Vale_RFC "Ebbw Vale RFC") and made his debut for the region in December 2007, coming off the bench away to [Munster](/wiki/Munster_Rugby "Munster Rugby"). He would go on to make a further 14 appearances for the Newport Gwent Dragons gaining [Celtic League](/wiki/Pro14 "Pro14"), [LV Cup](/wiki/Anglo-Welsh_Cup "Anglo-Welsh Cup") and four [Heineken Cup](/wiki/Heineken_Cup "Heineken Cup") experiences. Lewis scored a try in the Christmas period derby win over the [Ospreys](/wiki/Ospreys_(rugby_union) "Ospreys (rugby union)").[[1]](#cite_note-1)

In May 2009 Lewis joined [London Welsh](/wiki/London_Welsh_RFC "London Welsh RFC") and made a total of 88 appearances in three seasons and in 2011–2012 he played a major role in London Welsh winning the English Championship, beating Cornish Pirates in the two leg final and gaining promotion to the Aviva Premiership. During Lewis’s first spell at London Welsh RFC he represented The Barbarians, helping them to a narrow victory over the Combined Services at Aldershot RFC.

In July 2012 he joined [Cardiff Blues](/wiki/Cardiff_Blues "Cardiff Blues")[[2]](#cite_note-2) but in December he returned to London Welsh who were then competing in the Premiership.[[3]](#cite_note-3) Lewis again played a major role in London Welsh winning the English Championship for a second time in 2013–2014 and went on to play in a total of 136 league games making him the second highest in league appearances in the club's history after Matt Corker.

Following the unfortunate financial situation at London Welsh and its loss of a licence to continue to play in the RFU Greene King Championship, Rob returned to Ebbw Vale RFC in January 2017.[[4]](#cite_note-4) On 16 March 2017 [Birmingham Moseley Rugby Club](/wiki/Birmingham_Moseley_Rugby_Club "Birmingham Moseley Rugby Club") announced that Lewis was the first new signing for the club's 2017–2018 [National League 1](/wiki/National_League_1 "National League 1") campaign. In his second season at Birmingham Moseley Lewis was announced as Captain, alongside Buster Lawrence and Sam Brown.

After a two season spell in Birmingham Lewis would go on to join Luctonians RFC, whilst also becoming Head Coach at [Malvern College](/wiki/Malvern_College "Malvern College").[[5]](#cite_note-5)

In September 2020 Lewis was appointed as the Head of Rugby at [Royal Grammar School Worcester](/wiki/Royal_Grammar_School_Worcester "Royal Grammar School Worcester").[[6]](#cite_note-6). Lewis, alongside his coaching team consisting of [Aleki Lutui](/wiki/Aleki_Lutui "Aleki Lutui") and [Ollie Allsopp](/wiki/Ollie_Allsopp "Ollie Allsopp") coached RGS to [Modus Cup](/wiki/Modus_Cup "Modus Cup") success in 2022, winning 20-17 [[7]](#cite_note-7), and in 2025, winning 21-20. [[8]](#cite_note-8). On 12th May 2025, Lewis was appointed Director of Senior Rugby at National League Two West club [Luctonians](/wiki/Luctonians "Luctonians"), where he used to play.

## Personal

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Robert_Lewis_(rugby_union)&action=edit&section=2 "Edit section: Personal")]

Robert's twin brother [James Lewis](/wiki/James_Lewis_(rugby_player) "James Lewis (rugby player)") is also a professional rugby union player.
